sitting_shapes: Beat-structured spine (6-8 beats per sitting, proven by walk_hs85_live.py's 6 and Phase-87/90's 8): each beat is one atomic instruction->expectation->verdict, numbered, printed as it runs; a sitting is a linear ordered march, never a free-form checklist. The dogfood PROTOCOL's flat ~58-check list is the anti-pattern the site replaces — walks that closed phases were always short ordered beat runs.

Stage-then-walk: every walk opens by inducing the world (start_worker, import a FRESH transcript, arm a grant, spawn a pane) and asserting it live before the first human beat — the UAT recipe+verify-probe is exactly this preamble lifted out of each hand-rolled script's top.

Control-vs-treatment as a first-class beat for every LLM feature: run the SAME prompt with grounding OFF then ON and assert the treatment output names a repo-specific token the control cannot know (hs88: 'steer_walk_hs87' present in treatment, absent in control; hs87: 'BLUEBIRD'/'Friday the 13th 3:47pm' vs 'I don't have access'). A no-LLM pass alone is declared insufficient — it hides broken features.

Honest-failure beat closes the sitting: deliberately break the world mid-run (kill the worker, recycle the pane, dead endpoint) and assert the product degrades correctly and FAST — offline door reads offline, refusal returns <5s naming the node, no hang. 'Refuse fast by name' is the pass bar, not 'it worked'.

Structural (not exact-wording) verdicts for non-deterministic output: assert an artifact of the right TYPE exists and rendered (mermaid <svg>, action-item checklist, a decision OR open-question), judged on substance — did it surface the decision/owner/risk — never on literal text (spoken_meeting_e2e's whole assertion philosophy).

Every beat is a capture: a screenshot into the phase screenshots dir AND a printed provenance line (badge text, refs, worker completions before->after) so the beat proves execution-location, not just a green tick. The badge ('mesh . walk-edge', 'On-device', egress scope) is asserted content.

Freshness discipline: NEVER replay against a fixed fixture — reroute/intel dedup off the transcript hash, so a fresh timestamped transcript per run is minted or the whole beat silently no-ops ('deduped'). Recipes must produce novel state each application or idempotently re-verify, never falsely pass on stale cache.

Isolation preamble + teardown finally-block: run under an isolated HOME (dogfood _home pattern), capture prior assignments, and in a finally: restore assignments, delete the walk's recipe/profile/meeting, kill the worker's process GROUP (start_new_session so `uv run` children don't orphan). A sitting must leave zero residue and never touch the real ~/.config.

Press-play device runbook shape (HSM-16-06): a pre-flight block (hub bound for LAN, device paired host+port+token, build installed on UNLOCKED device with the headless-signing recipe), then N labelled checks each with EXPECT + an explicit CONTROL (bin the second ask -> nothing appears), and a fill-in trace table at the bottom. Time-boxed (~15 min) and explicitly a bug-hunt too.
recipe_candidates: fresh-desk: boot golden-local under an isolated HOME, no seeds; probe: /api/desk empty, doctor green (the dogfood setup.sh + hs doctor preamble).
seeded-desk: apply notes/KB/recipes through public /api/desk routes; probe: the seeded primitives read back and are indistinguishable from user-made (dogfood mock-repo .hs/ + KB, re-applied via API).

meeting-just-ended-open-actions: import a FRESH timestamped transcript through /api/meetings/import, run intel, probe >=1 open action via aftercare/history route (walk_hs85 beat 4 + spoken_meeting_e2e's import->plugins->persist chain).
intel-endpoint-dead: boot bad-endpoint deck (dead port); probe: doctor names the dead endpoint, a forced run refuses <5s, no hang (hs85 beat 6 + dogfood P-03/T2-11 posture).
first-run-no-model: boot no-model deck; probe: /welcome fronts, first-dictation path honest, milestone flips (dogfood X-04).

mesh-node-alive: spawn a real `holdspeak mesh serve` worker in its own process group with its own .43-wired HOME; probe: doctor 'Mesh edges' line reads live, /api/models shows the node (walk_hs85 start_worker + beat 1).
mesh-node-just-died: mid-run action — SIGINT the worker's process group, wait out the liveness window; probe: /api/models row live==false, ask refuses <5s naming the node (walk_hs85 beat 6).
agent-pane-awaiting-input: tmux new-session -d + arm a consent grant on the pinned %N; probe: peek_pane shows the prompt, grant live (rails_walk_hs88 beat 3 + Phase-87 SessionPullout).

grounded-ask-control-treatment: mint a repo-specific secret into a desk object, ask same prompt with grounding off/on; probe: treatment names the secret, control cannot (hs87 BLUEBIRD, hs88 filename — the reusable control/treatment rig).
spawned-then-killed-session: spawn via coder_factory, rename, kill through the steer gate; probe: audit reads back who/when/what/where, session gone (Phase-90 8-beat factory walk).

rendered-audio-meeting: `say`-render a scenario WAV via make_fixtures.py, import through Whisper with intel off; probe: transcript recognizable vs the .script.txt ground truth (dogfood T1-18 + make_fixtures substrate).
harness_gaps: Repeatability: every walk hand-rolled its own staging inline (start_worker, import-fresh-transcript, arm-grant) with no reusable named recipe or idempotency contract — two runs a month apart are not guaranteed comparable. The UAT recipe layer with a verify probe + idempotency (re-seed detection, not duplicate desks) is exactly what the scripts lacked.

Per-surface verdicts: every existing artifact proved ONE surface. spoken_meeting_e2e and walk_hs85 are web-only (Playwright on 127.0.0.1); HSM-16-06 is the lone cross-surface runbook but is a hand-filled markdown trace with no shared record. Nothing casts a verdict PER surface into a shared run so a web-pass/iPhone-fail split becomes one finding wearing both verdicts.

Human-forcing + structured feedback capture: dogfood PROTOCOL.md is a fillable markdown that 'sat on the shelf' (the UAT vision says so outright); the walk scripts are agent-run self-proofs with no human in the loop and no note/screenshot-per-step capture landing in a queryable DB.

The harness standing OUTSIDE the product: spoken_meeting_e2e imports holdspeak in-process (PluginHost, MeetingWebServer) — it cannot boot the product broken, kill it, and reboot it differently. walk_hs85 got closer (real subprocess worker) but still drove the owner's live hub, not an isolated bootable run. The conductor's subprocess-only boundary (grep-enforced no-import) is the missing capability.

Coverage accounting: no artifact could state what FRACTION of the 90-phase surface a run touched or what has NEVER been sat through — coverage was remembered, not enumerated against a ledger. The feature ledger + coverage math is net-new.

Log-slice correlation for debrief: walks print ad-hoc lines; none windowed the product's own log around a failing step and handed it to triage as the head start. The debrief packet's time-windowed log slice per non-pass verdict is new.

Device state induction: the walks induced HUB state but had no story for device-local state — HSM-16-06 relied on manual pairing/build/install steps a human performs. Inducing hub state induces synced device state, but anything device-local (an installed GGUF, airplane mode) is still hand-staged.

Findings lifecycle: walks either passed or the script failed loudly — there was no triage vocabulary (fix/wont-fix/by-design/duplicate), no finding IDs, no backlog-block generation feeding pm/roadmap/holdspeak/BACKLOG.md with the run as citation.
phase3_shape: Group packs by SHARED STAGED WORLD, not by feature area — a sitting's cost is dominated by staging, so each induced world must buy several scenarios (HSU-2-05's explicit directive). One pack == one (or one composed set of) recipe(s) applied once, then 6-8 scenarios walked over it.

Pack A — Meeting Aftercare (recipe: meeting-just-ended-open-actions on golden-43): import->intel artifacts, aftercare digest + moment-jump, action->issue propose/approve, egress badge scope. Must-tests first (the intel chain + open-action visibility), then spot-checks (export formats). ~40 min. Three-surface where the aftercare surface exists; n/a with reason where a surface lacks it.

Pack B — The Steering Desk (recipe: agent-pane-awaiting-input + spawned-then-killed-session): watch pull-out, hold-to-arm, key palette (C-c), steer with grounded desk object (control-vs-treatment beat), spawn/rename/kill through the gate, audit read-back. Web is reference; iPad mirrors (Phase-26 client); iPhone n/a-with-reason for the factory verbs. ~45 min.

Pack C — Dictation Grounding (recipe: seeded-desk with the three mock repos' .hs/+KB): per-repo grounded rewrite (ledgerline files+invariants, questline flag+WAQC, pylon rollback), spoken-symbols, the learning loop (correct->learned-from-N chip), languages. Control-vs-treatment on grounding is the spine. ~40 min.

Pack D — Honest Failure & Trust (recipes: intel-endpoint-dead, first-run-no-model, mesh-node-just-died): the degrade-fast beats — doctor names the dead endpoint, refusal <5s by name, offline door, first-run truth at N=0, egress badge correctness. This is the pack that proves the product is honest when broken. ~30 min. Deliberately short and must-test-heavy.

Pack E — The Mesh Edge (recipe: mesh-node-alive on golden-43): assign intel+dictation+an agent onto the edge profile, prove the run moves and the key/model don't (worker-completion delta + mesh badge), then kill it (folds into Pack D's failure discipline if time-boxed together). ~35 min.

Every pack MUST open with a staging-verify beat (the recipe's probe, shown honestly with log tail on failure) and CLOSE with at least one honest-failure or control beat — mirroring how every closed-phase walk both proved the happy path and broke the world. No pack is all-green-happy-path.

Ordering logic within a pack: must-tests first (a capability the parity claim depends on), should-tests next, spot-checks last, so a time-boxed sitting that runs short still cleared the load-bearing verdicts. Skips are declared in the pack, as explicit as musts (HSU-2-05 ranking vocabulary).

Keep the fully-local path in at least one pack (Pack D's first-run/no-model legs need no .43) so the rig demos and Phase-3 can be sat without the LAN — the dogfood 'at least one fully local recipe' rule carried forward.

Sitting size ~30-45 min each maps to 6-8 beats at ~4-5 min/beat including the open-product + cast-per-surface-verdict + screenshot overhead — the empirically-proven walk length (hs85=6, Phase-87/90=8, HSM-16-06=4 checks in ~15 min for a lighter device walk).
